title: Is Awareness Enough
justification: This talk directly addresses the chapter theme in its title and systematically explores whether awareness alone leads to enlightenment. It provides the most comprehensive treatment by examining the Buddha's own discovery, the seven factors of enlightenment, the relationship between sati and paññā, and how different traditions (Vipassana, Dzogchen, Zazen, Koan) approach this question. The talk offers rich examples, clear structure, and resolves the apparent contradiction between traditions with scholarly depth while remaining accessible.
summary: Bhante Bodhidhamma explores whether awareness alone is sufficient for enlightenment, drawing on the Buddha's biography and the Satipatthana discourse to show that right awareness must include intuitive intelligence (paññā) and investigation. He reconciles different Buddhist traditions' approaches, demonstrating they all require this combination of awareness with active inquiry into the three characteristics of existence.

relevance.note: Directly addresses if awareness alone sufficient for awakening
